sections.title: Core Website Setup
sections.description: Lock the production environment, customer-facing basics, and technical hygiene before traffic starts.

items.name: Production config and environment freeze
items.description: Verify production API URLs, payment/COD flags, image domains, email sender settings, and secret keys are correct in deployment config with a documented release snapshot.
items.notes: Confirm Next.js public env values against Django settings and document final launch values in ops notes.

items.name: SSL and mixed content check
items.description: Run the live site through HTTPS-only navigation and confirm no HTTP assets, insecure scripts, or image warnings appear on homepage, PDP, cart, checkout, and policy pages.
items.notes: Check CDN image URLs and any hardcoded asset links from older templates.

items.name: Responsive layout and navigation pass
items.description: Test header, footer, search, category navigation, cart drawer/page, and checkout layout on mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints with real content.
items.notes: Focus on 360px to 430px widths where ecommerce layouts usually break first.

items.name: Broken links and fallback page coverage
items.description: Check all primary nav links, homepage banners, category links, PDP breadcrumbs, cart routes, 404 page, and error boundaries so users never hit a dead end during launch week.
items.notes: Include manual checks for banners, footer links, and empty-state CTAs.

sections.title: Product Management
sections.description: Ensure products are launch-ready, complete, and operational in both storefront and admin.

items.name: Product image quality and consistency
items.description: Review every launch SKU for sharp primary images, clean background, consistent aspect ratio, zoom-ready resolution, and no supplier watermarks or cropped variants.
items.notes: Target at least 1200px square exports for main catalog images.

items.name: Required catalog attributes complete
items.description: Confirm title, slug, category, MRP, selling price, COD eligibility, stock, brand/specs, shipping promise, and return eligibility exist for each listed product.
items.notes: Use admin export to spot blank brand/spec fields before enabling more SKUs.

items.name: Stock handling and out-of-stock behavior
items.description: Validate admin stock edits update storefront availability, low-stock items are visible internally, and out-of-stock products cannot be purchased while still showing useful alternatives.
items.notes: Verify low stock threshold and disable purchase CTA when quantity reaches zero.

items.name: PDP merchandising and trust content
items.description: Make sure each product detail page shows price breakdown, COD messaging, delivery estimate, return cue, highlights/specifications, and visible support path near the purchase action.
items.notes: Audit the top 20 launch SKUs manually because template issues surface there first.

sections.title: Checkout and Order Flow
sections.description: Stress the full cart-to-order path with COD-first logic, operational handoffs, and error handling.

items.name: Cart, shipping, and order total accuracy
items.description: Test quantity changes, coupon edge cases, shipping fee rules, and final order totals so cart, checkout, and admin order records match exactly.
items.notes: Reconcile subtotal, shipping, COD fee if any, and final payable amount in both UI and order payload.

items.name: COD verification and risk control flow
items.description: Validate COD-eligible order creation, customer verification steps, blocked/soft-block customer rules, and internal handling for suspicious repeat-RTO customers.
items.notes: Define who confirms COD orders and where verification status is tracked before dispatch.

items.name: Order confirmation workflow
items.description: Confirm successful checkout triggers thank-you page state, admin order entry, confirmation email/SMS/WhatsApp plan, and clear next-step messaging for the customer.
items.notes: Existing email flow passes tests; still validate live copy and sender identity on production.

items.name: Checkout validation and error recovery
items.description: Test invalid phone numbers, partial addresses, unavailable items, network failures, and page refreshes to make sure checkout recovers gracefully without duplicate orders.
items.notes: Include back-button behavior and retry after session expiration.

sections.title: SEO Setup
sections.description: Cover crawlability, metadata, schema, and social previews for search-ready launch pages.

items.name: Title and meta description templates
items.description: Set production-ready title and description patterns for homepage, categories, products, cart-safe pages, and policy pages without duplicate or placeholder copy.
items.notes: Keep product titles readable and capped for search results, not just admin naming.

items.name: Product schema on PDP
items.description: Implement and validate structured data with product name, image, price, availability, brand, aggregate rating if real, and offer information on product pages.
items.notes: Use only real ratings/reviews; do not publish fake aggregate data.

items.name: Sitemap and robots.txt validation
items.description: Confirm the generated sitemap includes indexable storefront URLs and robots.txt blocks only admin/internal paths while allowing product and category discovery.
items.notes: Re-submit final sitemap URL in Search Console after launch domain is fixed.

items.name: Open Graph, Twitter, and canonical tags
items.description: Check social preview tags and canonical URLs on homepage, category, and product pages so shared links render correctly and duplicates do not fragment ranking signals.
items.notes: Use a real product image for PDP previews and a default brand image for collection pages.

sections.title: Google Merchant / Free Listings Setup
sections.description: Prepare the catalog feed and account settings so products are eligible for free listings and future ads.

items.name: Merchant Center account, domain claim, and business info
items.description: Verify Merchant Center is created, website is claimed and verified, business details are complete, and shipping/return references point to live policy pages.
items.notes: Use the exact launch domain and match store branding with the site footer/business details.

items.name: Feed required attributes readiness
items.description: Ensure feed rows expose stable ID, title, description, link, image_link, availability, price, condition, brand, and shipping details where required for launch SKUs.
items.notes: Prepare fallback rules for products missing GTIN/MPN and document the identifier strategy.

items.name: Merchant shipping, returns, and policy alignment
items.description: Match Merchant Center shipping speeds, return window, and contact info with the actual customer-facing shipping and return policy pages on Vorionmart.
items.notes: Blocked until the final return window and shipping SLA are approved.

items.name: Feed diagnostics and sample product validation
items.description: Upload or fetch the feed, inspect diagnostics, and manually verify at least 10 sample SKUs in Merchant Center for landing-page parity and image approval.
items.notes: Include one SKU from each launch category instead of testing only best sellers.

sections.title: Legal and Trust
sections.description: Make policies, contact signals, and customer trust cues visible before traffic and orders arrive.

items.name: Shipping and return policy visibility
items.description: Place shipping and return/refund links in footer, checkout, and PDP trust zones so customers can find policy details before ordering.
items.notes: Verify policy links are not hidden behind expandable accordions on mobile.

items.name: Terms, privacy, and COD disclaimer consistency
items.description: Review terms, privacy, COD disclaimer, and return policy language so billing, cancellation, support, and data-use statements do not contradict each other.
items.notes: Specifically align COD cancellation and refusal wording across all policy pages.

items.name: Support contact visibility
items.description: Expose at least one verified email and phone/WhatsApp support path in footer, contact page, order confirmation, and return/help journeys.
items.notes: Use the same contact details across merchant listings, footer, and transactional templates.

items.name: Trust messaging near purchase actions
items.description: Show customer-safe cues like COD availability, secure checkout note, delivery expectations, and return promise adjacent to add-to-cart or checkout CTAs.
items.notes: Avoid clutter; keep trust content visible without pushing the buy box below the fold.

sections.title: Marketing Readiness
sections.description: Make sure launch campaigns can send traffic to valid pages with clear offers and usable assets.

items.name: Homepage hero and launch offer readiness
items.description: Confirm banners, homepage hero copy, offer badges, and category highlights match the products and pricing that will actually be live on launch day.
items.notes: Remove any placeholder sales language that is not backed by live catalog pricing.

items.name: Campaign landing page and deep-link readiness
items.description: Validate every marketing URL, UTM-tagged link, and influencer/ads destination leads to a live category, PDP, or launch page with no empty inventory.
items.notes: Create a simple source-of-truth sheet mapping campaign names to live URLs.

items.name: Coupon, offer, and COD incentive rules
items.description: Test any launch coupon, free-shipping threshold, bundle, or COD incentive logic so the promise shown in marketing creatives matches checkout behavior.
items.notes: Blocked until the final launch discount strategy is approved.

items.name: Launch creative and support asset pack
items.description: Prepare approved product images, resized ad creatives, social copy, FAQ snippets, and customer-support macros for the first 72 hours after launch.
items.notes: Include quick-reply answers for COD, delivery timing, and return eligibility.

sections.title: Analytics and Tracking
sections.description: Instrument the customer journey so launch traffic and order drop-offs can be diagnosed immediately.

items.name: GA4 and tag manager installation
items.description: Verify analytics containers are installed on all storefront routes with the correct production measurement IDs and no duplicate page_view firing.
items.notes: Check route transitions in Next.js so page views are not missed on client-side navigation.

items.name: Core ecommerce event tracking
items.description: Fire and validate view_item, add_to_cart, begin_checkout, add_shipping_info where applicable, and purchase/order_placed events with usable product and revenue payloads.
items.notes: For COD-first flow, ensure purchase/order_placed tracks after order creation, not after payment success.

items.name: Ads and remarketing pixel validation
items.description: Check Meta or other ad pixels, remarketing tags, and custom conversions on key pages so paid traffic can be optimized after launch.
items.notes: Only enable channels that have actual launch budget and consent-compliant usage.

items.name: Search Console and webmaster verification
items.description: Verify domain ownership, submit sitemap, and confirm index coverage can be monitored from day one after the public launch.
items.notes: Track the first crawl errors and soft-404s during the launch week.

sections.title: Admin and Operations
sections.description: Prepare the internal team, admin tools, and resilience processes required to fulfill real orders.

items.name: Admin roles and access review
items.description: Confirm only required users have access to products, orders, banners, settings, partners, and customer data, with launch-day operators clearly assigned.
items.notes: Remove unused test accounts and verify least-privilege access for non-founder users.

items.name: Admin stock handling workflow
items.description: Document who updates stock, how fast sell-outs are reflected, how cancelled COD inventory is returned to stock, and what backup process exists if admin updates fail.
items.notes: Create a launch-day stock escalation channel for hot SKUs.

items.name: Backup and security checks
items.description: Validate database backup schedule, media backup coverage, admin auth protections, secret management, and incident contact list before public launch.
items.notes: Include restore verification for at least one recent backup, not backup creation alone.

items.name: Order processing SOP and escalation path
items.description: Define a practical SOP covering COD verification, packing, dispatch, delayed shipment follow-up, cancellation handling, and return/refund ownership.
items.notes: Blocked until shipping partner cutoff times and return SLA are finalized.

sections.title: Final QA Testing
sections.description: Run the last launch rehearsal on real devices and production-like data so launch risk is known, not guessed.

items.name: Cross-device storefront smoke test
items.description: Run full browse-to-cart-to-order flows on at least one Android phone, one iPhone-sized viewport, one laptop browser, and one low-bandwidth session.
items.notes: Use production-like catalog images and real policy pages during the rehearsal.

items.name: COD order rehearsal with admin follow-through
items.description: Place a complete COD test order and verify it reaches admin, triggers confirmation steps, and can be moved through the first fulfillment states without manual patching.
items.notes: Use a dedicated test phone/email so communications can be verified end to end.

items.name: Browser console and performance sanity pass
items.description: Check for JavaScript errors, failed network requests, oversized images, and severe Core Web Vitals regressions on homepage, categories, PDP, cart, and checkout.
items.notes: Prioritize image-heavy PDPs and launch landing pages where LCP risk is highest.

items.name: Launch-day rollback and support plan
items.description: Prepare a clear owner list, issue triage channel, rollback trigger, backup contact sheet, and short customer-facing incident response template before going live.
items.notes: Keep the rollback decision simple: disable traffic, pause campaigns, and revert the release if checkout or order creation is unstable.
